sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a female with a history of coronary artery disease, hypertension, and diabetes, presented for follow-up after an interior STEMI where a stent was placed.
She reported experiencing similar symptoms to her previous heart attack, including chest pain that did not resolve on its own.
She denied experiencing chest pain or shortness of breath since her last visit six months ago and reported being able to exercise by walking her dog daily.
She mentioned noticing lower leg swelling since being discharged from the hospital and stated she has been taking her medications as prescribed.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of coronary artery disease, hypertension, and diabetes.
She has been prescribed metformin for her diabetes and is taking it as directed.
Review of systems: The patient reported no symptoms of chest pain or shortness of breath but did mention lower leg swelling.
Current medications:
Aspirin 81mg daily
Brilinta 90mg twice daily
Lipitor 80mg daily
Toprol 50mg daily
Metformin (dose not specified)
Lisinopril 20mg daily
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs were not specifically mentioned, so [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The doctor performed a physical exam and found no jugular venous distention or carotid bruits.
A 3/6 systolic ejection murmur was heard at the left base.
The patient's lungs were clear, but the doctor noted lower limb edema with pitting 1+ on the right radial artery.
The cath site was clean, dry, and intact without hematoma, and the patient's right radial artery pulses were palpable.
Test Results: The patient's EKG showed a normal sinus rhythm and good R wave progression, with evolutionary changes expected after a STEMI.
The echo revealed a reduced ejection fraction of 35%.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has coronary artery disease, newly diagnosed reduced left ventricular dysfunction and moderate mitral regurgitation, and acute heart failure.
Problem list: Coronary artery disease; reduced left ventricular dysfunction; moderate mitral regurgitation; acute heart failure; hypertension.
Plan:
I prescribed Lasix 40mg daily for fluid retention.
I added Aldactone 12.5mg daily.
I will refer the patient to cardiac rehab for education and exercise guidance.
I will continue the patient on Aspirin 81mg daily, Brilinta 90mg twice daily, Lipitor 80mg daily, Toprol 50mg daily, and Lisinopril 20mg daily.
I will repeat an echo in two months.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again in two months to follow up on the echo results and to monitor her condition.
